title: Cisco ACI Fault Lifecycle, MTTR, Stuck-Clear, and Storm Detection
exclusions: Cisco Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller (NDFC) LAN fabrics without APIC fault MO families; VMware NSX alarm lifecycles (cat-18.2.*); Catalyst Center / DNAC faults (cat-15); UCS Manager fault hierarchies (cat-19); pure volume-only fault dashboards already covered by UC-18.1.2 without per-fault state tracking.
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) requiring five distinct sourcetypes: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** (REST **`/api/class/faultInst.json`** plus optional **`faultDelegate`** pulls aligned in the same stream), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault_record`** (**`/api/class/faultRecord.json`** historical clears), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (**Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** mnemonic fault strings), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR** acknowledgement and edit trail), **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** (fabric health snapshots that contextualize elongated **`mttr_min`**) ‚ÄĒ poll intervals typically **120‚Äď300s** for MO pulls and **60s‚Äď5m** for health, volume scales with fabric object counts.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** **4022**, install directory **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**) for **`cisco:aci:syslog`** normalization plus companion REST collectors; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase** **1546**) for **`faultInst`**, **`faultRecord`**, **`faultDelegate`**, and **audit** JSON; optional **Cisco DC Networking for Splunk Platform** (**cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk**, **Splunkbase** **7777**) for prebuilt ACI panels once indexes align.
spl:
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault_record") earliest=-7d@m latest=now
| eval lc_state=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,fault_lc,"")))
| eval code_u=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,"")))
| eval dn_k=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,"")))
| eval apic_id=lower(trim(coalesce(apic,apic_name,host,"fabric")))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,faultSeverity,"")))
| eval ts_raise=coalesce(strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval ts_last=coalesce(strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval occur_n=tonumber(coalesce(occur,"1"))
| eval delegated_f=lower(trim(coalesce(delegated,"no")))
| eval is_delegate=if(delegated_f="yes" OR delegated_f="true",1,0)
| eval mo_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,mo_class,object_type,"faultInst")))
| eval rule_tag=coalesce(rule,"")
| eval change_snip=substr(coalesce(changeSet,""),1,96)
| where isnotnull(code_u) AND code_u!="" AND isnotnull(dn_k) AND dn_k!=""
| eval in_clearing_lane=if(lc_state IN ("raised-clearing","retaining","retaining-acknowl"),1,0)
| eval is_cleared=if(lc_state="cleared" OR sev="cleared",1,0)
| eval mttr_sec=if((in_clearing_lane=1 OR is_cleared=1) AND isnotnull(ts_raise) AND isnotnull(ts_last), ts_last-ts_raise, null())
| eval hours_since_last=if(isnotnull(ts_last), round((now()-ts_last)/3600,2), null())
| eval stuck_in_raised=if(lc_state="raised" AND hours_since_last>24 AND match(sev,"(critical|major)"),1,0)
| bin span=5m _time
| eventstats count as raises_in_5m by apic_id _time
| stats latest(lc_state) as lifecycle latest(sev) as severity latest(mo_hint) as mo_class latest(ts_raise) as created_ts latest(ts_last) as last_transition_ts latest(rule_tag) as rule_name latest(change_snip) as change_excerpt max(mttr_sec) as mttr_seconds max(hours_since_last) as hours_since_trans max(stuck_in_raised) as stuck_open max(raises_in_5m) as burst_peak max(is_delegate) as delegation_seen max(occur_n) as occur_max latest(cause) as cause_latest by dn_k code_u
| eval mttr_min=if(isnotnull(mttr_seconds), round(mttr_seconds/60,2), null())
| eval fault_storm=if(burst_peak>50,1,0)
| eval recurring=if(occur_max>5,1,0)
| eval never_cleared_story=if(lifecycle="raised" AND stuck_open=1,1,0)
| where stuck_open=1 OR fault_storm=1 OR recurring=1 OR never_cleared_story=1 OR (isnotnull(mttr_min) AND mttr_min>=15 AND match(severity,"(critical|major)"))
| sort 0 - stuck_open - fault_storm - mttr_seconds
| head 300
| table dn_k code_u lifecycle mo_class severity mttr_min hours_since_trans stuck_open fault_storm recurring occur_max delegation_seen rule_name change_excerpt cause_latest created_ts last_transition_ts
description: Tracks individual **Cisco ACI** **faultInst** / **faultRecord** managed objects through **lc** lifecycles (**soaking**, **raised**, **raised-clearing**, **retaining**, **retaining-acknowl**, **cleared**) to compute **MTTR**-style durations from **`created`** through clearing transitions, flag **raised** faults stale beyond **24h**, detect coordinated **fault storms** (**>50** raises inside **5** minutes per **APIC** node), and highlight recurring **code**+**dn** pairs with **`occur`** spikes‚ÄĒcomplementing UC-18.1.2 severity volume analytics without duplicating raw count dashboards.
value: Leadership reporting gains trustworthy **repair-interval** narratives tied to real controller state machines instead of cherry-picked counters‚ÄĒaccelerating escalations when faults linger in **raised**, justifying capacity work when bursts cluster by **APIC**, and giving problem management evidence when the same **MO** path rejects fixes repeatedly as **occur** climbs.
implementation: Land **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** syslog plus **rest_ta** pulls for **`faultInst`**, **`faultRecord`**, optional **`faultDelegate`**, and **aaaModLR** audits into **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:fault_record`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, and **`cisco:aci:health`** indexes; normalize **FIELDALIAS** for **lc** and **lastTransition**; schedule lifecycle, MTTR, stuck-clear, and storm saved searches with change-aware throttles.
visualization: (1) **Timeline** or **timechart** of **lc** transitions per **dn** beside **health** lanes. (2) **Histogram** of **mttr_min** for **critical**/**major** cohorts. (3) Table ranking **stuck_open**, **fault_storm**, **recurring**. (4) **Single-value** medians for **MTTR** with drilldowns to **rule**/**changeSet** excerpts. (5) Optional heatmap of **delegation_seen** bursts by **tenant**.
schema: CIM **Alerts** supplies normalized **severity**, **signature**, and lifecycle-friendly **vendor_product** fields for correlation-ready dashboards; **Change** covers acknowledgement and maintenance metadata pulled from **aaaModLR** and ITSM lookups that explains bursts alongside **fault_storm** detections.

references.title: Cisco APIC Faults, Events, and System Messages Management Guide
Cisco APIC Object Model Reference ‚ÄĒ faultInst
Cisco DC Networking for Splunk Platform (Splunkbase 7777)

knownFalsePositives:
**lc_poll_blind_spot** ‚ÄĒ Sub-minute soaking churn or rapid raised-clearing oscillations between REST polls can hide transitions that syslog shows, so Splunk may under-count dwell time unless you shorten rest_ta intervals or blend cisco:aci:syslog narratives for the same code and dn. Pair shortened polls with Splunk-side max(_time) freshness checks so silent collectors do not masquerade as healthy fabrics.

**retention_timer_rollover** ‚ÄĒ faultRecord rows age out per fvFaultLifecycleP while faultInst snapshots still look hot; analysts treat short historical silence as clearance when the MO simply aged out of the history class pull. Cross-check moquery history against Splunk retention before closing executive summaries.

**delegate_double_count** ‚ÄĒ Summing parent faultDelegate rows with child faultInst volumes double-charges severity during storms; segment dashboards by MO class before interpreting storm heuristics. Teach operators that delegation bubbles describe blast summaries, not independent defects.

**ack_automation_skew** ‚ÄĒ Service accounts push acknowledgement fields in aaaModLR trails, inflating human MTTA stories unless scripted identities are excluded via lookups. Maintain a service-account suppression table reviewed quarterly.

**fault_storm_maintenance** ‚ÄĒ APIC maintenance windows and simultaneous line-card reloads legitimately raise dozens of raised faults within minutes; correlate _time with change tickets before paging on storm logic alone. Encode maintenance identifiers into lookup tables that temporarily raise suppression thresholds.

**never_cleared_stale_snapshot** ‚ÄĒ A stalled modular input replays the same JSON payload; lifecycle stays raised in Splunk even when APIC cleared hours ago until collectors refresh‚ÄĒwatch for frozen lastTransition timestamps across sequential polls. Alert when _time gaps exceed twice the configured poll interval without credential errors.

**raised_clearing_flap** ‚ÄĒ Interfaces bouncing during optics work can park faults in raised-clearing and retaining, producing short mttr_min readings while occur climbs‚ÄĒpair clearance math with occur counters and physical validation. Document optics work orders beside Splunk timelines to defuse false closure narratives.

**change_set_echo** ‚ÄĒ Automation repeatedly posts benign changeSet deltas that momentarily touch lc without true clearance, creating MTTR noise until you filter scripted subjects through an allowlist. Require human-linked tickets for closure when changeSet subjects match automation patterns.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚ÄĘ APIC 6.0(x) / 6.1(x) clusters expose **`/api/class/faultInst.json`**, **`/api/class/faultRecord.json`**, and **`/api/class/faultDelegate.json`** over HTTPS 443/tcp‚ÄĒprovision read-only REST operators with RBAC scoped to read fv and infra MIT branches used by your collectors, not full admin personas.
‚ÄĘ Install Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI packaged as **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (**Splunkbase** 4022) where APIC and Nexus syslog terminate so **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** carries mnemonic bursts that align with **`faultInst`** code families (F0103, F0467, F0613, F1394) without replacing lc truth from REST.
‚ÄĘ Add Splunk_REST_API_Modular_Input (**rest_ta**, **Splunkbase** 1546) GET templates for **`/api/class/faultInst.json`** (live MO rows), **`/api/class/faultRecord.json`** (historical clears), optional **`/api/class/faultDelegate.json`** (delegation chains), and **`/api/class/aaaModLR.json`** or audit feeds mirroring POST acknowledgements‚ÄĒtarget **`index=cisco_aci`** (rename per governance) and sourcetypes **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:fault_record`**, **`cisco:aci:audit`**, aligning **FIELDALIAS** maps for camelCase drift on **`created`**, **`lastTransition`**, `severity`, `lc`, `occur`, `changeSet`, `rule`, and `delegated`.
‚ÄĘ Poll **faultInst** every 120‚Äď300s on busy fabrics to balance APIC CPU with lifecycle fidelity; faster cadence improves soaking detection, slower cadence risks smoothing **raised-clearing** flaps that syslog still shouts about.
‚ÄĘ Map **fvFaultLifecycleP** defaults (roughly five-day retention for cleared records) versus per-code **`fvFaultLcP`** soak overrides so retaining versus cleared timestamps you compute as MTT endpoints match the controller‚ÄĒnot an assumed clock.
‚ÄĘ Confirm Splunk indexers, heavy forwarders, and credential vaults honor TLS without inspection boxes that rewrite JSON payloads mid-stream.

Step 1 ‚ÄĒ Configure data collection for lifecycle fidelity (not volume alone)
‚ÄĘ Live fault lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** via **`GET /api/class/faultInst.json`** with optional **`query-target-filter`** predicates to trim noisy tenants during labs‚ÄĒeach poll should preserve **`dn`**, **`code`**, `severity`, `lc`, `cause`, **`created`**, **`lastTransition`**, `occur`, `changeSet`, `rule`, and `delegated` attributes exactly as APIC returns them.
‚ÄĘ History lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault_record`** via **`GET /api/class/faultRecord.json`** using time-bounded **`created`** filters after UTC normalization‚ÄĒuse this lane to prove cleared outcomes that disappeared from **faultInst** yet remain operationally argumentative in reviews.
‚ÄĘ Syslog lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** from TA parsing‚ÄĒpair with REST because PRI shifts alone cannot reconstruct **soaking ‚Üí raised ‚Üí raised-clearing ‚Üí retaining ‚Üí retaining-acknowl** ordering.
‚ÄĘ Audit lane: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR**) capturing acknowledgement POST bodies with **`ackUser`**, **`ackCause`**, **`ackTime`** equivalents‚ÄĒrequired if you report human time-to-ack alongside **MTTR** from lifecycle transitions.
‚ÄĘ Health lane (correlation): **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:health`** from fabric score snapshots‚ÄĒuseful when **MTTR** elongations coincide with spine saturation even if fault codes differ across MO classes.

Step 2 ‚ÄĒ Create the search and alert
Paste after confirming five sourcetypes land in **`index=cisco_aci`** with fields populated‚ÄĒextend **`coalesce()`** chains if collectors renamed keys during TA upgrades.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault_record") earliest=-7d@m latest=now
| eval lc_state=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,fault_lc,"")))
| eval code_u=upper(trim(coalesce(code,fault_code,faultCode,"")))
| eval dn_k=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,"")))
| eval apic_id=lower(trim(coalesce(apic,apic_name,host,"fabric")))
| eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,faultSeverity,"")))
| eval ts_raise=coalesce(strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(created,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval ts_last=coalesce(strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),strptime(lastTransition,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"))
| eval occur_n=tonumber(coalesce(occur,"1"))
| eval delegated_f=lower(trim(coalesce(delegated,"no")))
| eval is_delegate=if(delegated_f="yes" OR delegated_f="true",1,0)
| eval mo_hint=lower(trim(coalesce(classId,mo_class,object_type,"faultInst")))
| eval rule_tag=coalesce(rule,"")
| eval change_snip=substr(coalesce(changeSet,""),1,96)
| where isnotnull(code_u) AND code_u!="" AND isnotnull(dn_k) AND dn_k!=""
| eval in_clearing_lane=if(lc_state IN ("raised-clearing","retaining","retaining-acknowl"),1,0)
| eval is_cleared=if(lc_state="cleared" OR sev="cleared",1,0)
| eval mttr_sec=if((in_clearing_lane=1 OR is_cleared=1) AND isnotnull(ts_raise) AND isnotnull(ts_last), ts_last-ts_raise, null())
| eval hours_since_last=if(isnotnull(ts_last), round((now()-ts_last)/3600,2), null())
| eval stuck_in_raised=if(lc_state="raised" AND hours_since_last>24 AND match(sev,"(critical|major)"),1,0)
| bin span=5m _time
| eventstats count as raises_in_5m by apic_id _time
| stats latest(lc_state) as lifecycle latest(sev) as severity latest(mo_hint) as mo_class latest(ts_raise) as created_ts latest(ts_last) as last_transition_ts latest(rule_tag) as rule_name latest(change_snip) as change_excerpt max(mttr_sec) as mttr_seconds max(hours_since_last) as hours_since_trans max(stuck_in_raised) as stuck_open max(raises_in_5m) as burst_peak max(is_delegate) as delegation_seen max(occur_n) as occur_max latest(cause) as cause_latest by dn_k code_u
| eval mttr_min=if(isnotnull(mttr_seconds), round(mttr_seconds/60,2), null())
| eval fault_storm=if(burst_peak>50,1,0)
| eval recurring=if(occur_max>5,1,0)
| eval never_cleared_story=if(lifecycle="raised" AND stuck_open=1,1,0)
| where stuck_open=1 OR fault_storm=1 OR recurring=1 OR never_cleared_story=1 OR (isnotnull(mttr_min) AND mttr_min>=15 AND match(severity,"(critical|major)"))
| sort 0 - stuck_open - fault_storm - mttr_seconds
| head 300
| table dn_k code_u lifecycle mo_class severity mttr_min hours_since_trans stuck_open fault_storm recurring occur_max delegation_seen rule_name change_excerpt cause_latest created_ts last_transition_ts
```

Understanding this SPL
‚ÄĘ State focus: unlike **UC-18.1.2** volume dashboards, this pipeline keeps **`lifecycle`** (lc) per `dn_k` + `code_u` and derives repair-interval style durations (**`mttr_min`**) when the fault enters **`raised-clearing`**, `retaining`, or **`retaining-acknowl`** lanes (including `cleared` severity rows from **`cisco:aci:fault_record`**).
‚ÄĘ Stuck logic flags `raised` critical/major rows whose **`lastTransition`** is older than 24h, signaling remediation drift or a stalled collector.
‚ÄĘ Fault storm logic bins `_time` into 5-minute buckets per `apic_id`; more than 50 events in a window points to coordinated raises deserving a bridge‚ÄĒnot a lone line card.
‚ÄĘ Recurring faults use **`occur_max > 5`** to spotlight chronic **`code`**, **`dn`** pairs even when severity looks modest individually.
‚ÄĘ **`delegation_seen`** warns when `delegated` attributes mark faults bubbled from child MO paths‚ÄĒexclude double counts in downstream KPI math.
‚ÄĘ Tune **`fault_storm`** thresholds per pod density; **`mttr_min>=15`** filter trims noise for major/critical only‚ÄĒrelax for labs.

Pipeline walkthrough (selected stages)
‚ÄĘ Scopes **`index=cisco_aci`** across **`cisco:aci:fault`** and **`cisco:aci:fault_record`** so both active **faultInst** and historical clears influence **MTTR** math.
‚ÄĘ Normalizes **`lc_state`** to track soaking, raised, **raised-clearing**, retaining, **retaining-acknowl**, and cleared outcomes consistently across transports.
‚ÄĘ Derives **`mttr_sec`** by subtracting parsed **`created`** from **`lastTransition`** whenever the row sits in clearing or retained families‚ÄĒthis approximates operator-facing mean time to repair for automation-friendly cohorts.
‚ÄĘ Uses **`eventstats`** to compute **`raises_in_5m`** per APIC identity, implementing the >50 faults in five minutes storm heuristic on a per-node basis.
‚ÄĘ `stats` collapses repeated polls to the latest **`lifecycle`** snapshot per `dn_k`, `code_u`, while preserving max `occur`, delegation hints, rule, and `changeSet` snippets for runbook starters.

Step 3 ‚ÄĒ Validate against APIC UI truth
‚ÄĘ Open APIC ‚Üí System ‚Üí Faults and locate the same **`dn`**‚ÄĒcompare Lifecycle badge strings against Splunk **`lifecycle`**; mismatch usually means duplicated transports or stale modular input tokens.
‚ÄĘ Cross-check **`lastTransition`** in JSON from **`moquery`** against Splunk `last_transition_ts`; widespread skew hints NTP drift or frozen REST sessions stuck on **`aaaRefresh`** failures.
‚ÄĘ Pick one critical fault stuck in `raised`: confirm whether `occur` increments in APIC while Splunk shows static rows‚ÄĒif so, widen poll frequency or inspect 429 throttling in **`splunkd.log`** for **rest_ta**.
‚ÄĘ Compare **`cisco:aci:fault_record`** counts near retention boundaries with **`fvFaultLifecycleP`**‚ÄĒSplunk may still hold JSON after APIC pruned **faultRecord** objects from interactive panes.

Step 4 ‚ÄĒ Operationalize dashboards and runbooks
‚ÄĘ Dashboard row A: timeline of **`lifecycle`** transitions per `dn_k` with `timechart` overlays of **`cisco:aci:health`** scores to narrate customer impact beside fault codes.
‚ÄĘ Dashboard row B: table sorted by **`stuck_open`** then **`fault_storm`** with columns **`mttr_min`**, **`occur_max`**, **`delegation_seen`**, `rule_name`, `change_excerpt` for bridge readability.
‚ÄĘ Dashboard row C: single-value **`median(mttr_min)`** for critical cohorts plus count of **`never_cleared_story`** tickets awaiting owner assignment.
‚ÄĘ Publish saved searches for paging: (1) **`stuck_open=1`** for SEV-1 rotation, (2) **`fault_storm=1`** after maintenance correlation, (3) `recurring=1` for problem management reviews‚ÄĒthrottle using change lookup windows to avoid duplicate bridges.

Step 5 ‚ÄĒ Visualization, alert design, and troubleshooting
‚ÄĘ Visualization: pair duration histograms of **`mttr_min`** with scatter plots of `hours_since_trans` versus **`occur_max`** to separate flaky optics from one-off misconfigurations; add sankey-style exports offline (Splunk natively favors timeline) by exporting **`transaction`** results for executive summaries.
‚ÄĘ Alert design: include `dn_k`, `code_u`, **`lifecycle`**, `severity`, `cause_latest`, and direct MIT hyperlinks in the payload; route delegation heavy storms to tenant owners using extracted `uni/tn-` segments via `rex` in downstream macros.
‚ÄĘ Troubleshooting: No fresh `_time`‚ÄĒverify **rest_ta** endpoint, password rotation, and TLS trust stores; **`lifecycle` blank**‚ÄĒcheck **FIELDALIAS** after TA upgrades; **`mttr_min` always null**‚ÄĒconfirm **`created`** parses (some exports omit fractional seconds); **`fault_storm` never fires**‚ÄĒvalidate five-minute bin alignment versus timezone overrides; duplicate rows‚ÄĒdedupe `dn_k`, `code_u`, `last_transition_ts` fingerprints when webhook plus REST replay the same snapshot; health lane empty‚ÄĒreenable **`cisco:aci:health`** poller tied to **`HDfabricOverallHealth5min-0`** style endpoints if your TA version supports it.

Anchors ‚ÄĒ **faultInst**, **faultRecord**, **faultDelegate**, lc, severity, created, **lastTransition**, occur, **retaining-acknowl**, **raised-clearing**, **MTTR**, MTTA, Splunk_TA_cisco-aci, **rest_ta**.
grandmaExplanation: We stitch each trouble item's stages from first flicker through clear-out, so crews spot tickets frozen halfway or never finishing‚ÄĒnot just how many warnings flash at once.
hardware: Cisco APIC controllers (M3/M4/L4): Nexus 9000 family leaf/spine nodes in ACI mode for MO subjects; optional Nexus Dashboard Orchestrator for multi-site narratives outside a single APIC cluster.
